scope_note: Note 2: Ch.60 includes fabrics made of metal thread used in apparel/furnishing. Note 3: throughout the Nomenclature, 'knitted' goods INCLUDES stitch-bonded goods where chain stitches are formed of textile yarn. Subheading Note 1: subheading 6005.35 = insecticide-impregnated warp knit fabric of polyethylene monofilament or polyester multifilament (30–55 g/m², mesh 20–100 holes/cm², specific listed insecticides).

inclusions.description: Pile fabrics, including 'long pile' fabrics and terry fabrics, knitted or crocheted. EXCEPTION per Note 1(c): knitted/crocheted pile fabrics that are impregnated, coated, covered or laminated REMAIN in 60.01 (unlike other coated knit fabrics which go to Ch.59)
Knitted or crocheted fabrics of a width not exceeding 30 cm, containing by weight 5% or more of elastomeric yarn or rubber thread, other than those of heading 60.01
Knitted or crocheted fabrics of a width not exceeding 30 cm, other than those of heading 60.01 or 60.02
Knitted or crocheted fabrics of a width exceeding 30 cm, containing by weight 5% or more of elastomeric yarn or rubber thread, other than those of heading 60.01

Warp knit fabrics (including those made on galloon knitting machines), other than those of headings 60.01 to 60.04. Subheading 6005.35: warp knit fabrics of PE monofilament or PET multifilament (30–55 g/m², mesh 20–100 holes/cm²) impregnated/coated with listed insecticides (alpha-cypermethrin, chlorfenapyr, deltamethrin, lambda-cyhalothrin, permethrin, pirimiphos-methyl)
Other knitted or crocheted fabrics (weft knit, crochet — residual for fabrics not in 60.01–60.05)

exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 58.04 (tulle and other net fabrics; lace in the piece) — Note 1(a). Crocheted lace fabric and net → 58.04, not Ch.60

exclusions.condition: Labels, badges and similar articles, knitted or crocheted, of heading 58.07
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 58.07 (labels, badges and similar articles of textile) — Note 1(b). Knitted/crocheted woven labels → 58.07

exclusions.condition: Knitted or crocheted fabrics that are impregnated, coated, covered or laminated — EXCEPT knitted/crocheted pile fabrics
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 59 (impregnated/coated/laminated textile fabrics) — Note 1(c). Coated knit base fabric → Ch.59. EXCEPTION: coated/impregnated PILE fabrics (terry, velour, long pile) stay in heading 60.01

classification_rules.rule: Note 3 — Definition of 'knitted': includes stitch-bonded goods with textile yarn chain stitches
classification_rules.description: Ch.60 (and the Nomenclature generally) interprets 'knitted' broadly to include STITCH-BONDED fabrics where the chain stitches are formed of TEXTILE YARN. This means certain technical non-woven-like structures that are held together by knitted yarn chains → classified as knitted fabrics. Plain bonded/glued non-wovens without yarn stitching → Ch.56 (56.03 non-wovens). Stitch-bonded fabrics with yarn chains → Ch.60.

classification_rules.rule: Note 1(c) exception — Coated pile fabrics stay in 60.01
classification_rules.description: A critical exception: normally, coated/impregnated/laminated knitted fabrics → Ch.59 (Note 1(c)). BUT knitted or crocheted PILE fabrics (terry, velour, long pile) that are impregnated/coated/laminated → REMAIN in heading 60.01. This is the only case where coating does not redirect knitted fabric from Ch.60 to Ch.59. For example: a terry knit fabric with waterproof laminate → 60.01. A plain jersey fabric with PU coating → Ch.59.

classification_rules.rule: Ch.60 classification matrix: width × elastomeric content × knit type
classification_rules.description: Ch.60 headings structured by three criteria: (1) WIDTH: ≤30 cm (narrow) → 60.02/60.03; >30 cm (wide) → 60.04/60.05/60.06. (2) ELASTOMERIC YARN/RUBBER THREAD content: ≥5% by weight → 60.02 (narrow) or 60.04 (wide). <5% elastomeric → 60.03 (narrow) or 60.05/60.06 (wide). (3) KNIT TYPE for wide fabric: WARP KNIT → 60.05. OTHER (weft knit, circular knit, crochet) → 60.06. Pile fabrics always → 60.01 (overrides width/elastomeric splits if pile character).

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: Ch.60 (knitted fabrics in piece) vs Ch.61 (made-up knitted garments) vs Ch.59 (coated technical fabrics)
classification_rules.description: Ch.60 covers knitted FABRICS in piece/roll form — not yet made up into garments. When knitted fabric is cut and assembled into garments → Ch.61. When knitted fabric is impregnated/coated/laminated (except pile) → Ch.59. The key sequence: raw knit yarn → Ch.54/55 (man-made) or Ch.51/52/53 (natural); knitted fabric in piece → Ch.60; coated knit fabric → Ch.59; made-up garment → Ch.61; worn used knit garment in bulk → Ch.63 (63.09).

classification_rules.rule: Subheading Note 1 — Insecticide-treated warp knit for mosquito nets (subheading 6005.35)
classification_rules.description: Subheading 6005.35 covers a very specific product: warp knit fabrics of POLYETHYLENE MONOFILAMENT or POLYESTER MULTIFILAMENT, weighing 30–55 g/m², mesh size 20–100 holes/cm², impregnated or coated with one of the LISTED INSECTICIDES (alpha-cypermethrin, chlorfenapyr, deltamethrin, lambda-cyhalothrin, permethrin, pirimiphos-methyl). This matches the base fabric for mosquito nets (the fabric before being made up into nets). Made-up mosquito nets from this fabric → Ch.63 (heading 63.04 subheading 6304.20).
